created_for: Recent-season pre-match information expansion and selective-prediction research

clubelo_via_soccerdata.reason: read_by_date(date) returns historical ratings at a specified date and rating validity intervals.

fbref_via_soccerdata.reason: Observed match/player stats are post-match labels; they may only influence later fixtures. Historical page availability timestamps are not established by the scraper.

sofascore_via_soccerdata.reason: The open scraper exposes schedules but does not establish original historical publication timestamps for every field.

sofifa_via_soccerdata.reason: Ratings have update/version dates. A value is eligible only when its update/version date is strictly before the target freeze and the historical version can be reproduced.

whoscored_via_soccerdata.reason: read_missing_players is valuable pre-match context, but the scraper output does not by itself prove the original historical observation timestamp. Current query-time snapshots can be frozen prospectively.

understat_via_soccerdata.reason: Completed-match xG and shots may be used only for later fixtures. Existing V5.1.3 recent-season holdout is closed and cannot be retuned.

gdelt_doc_2.reason: Article-list results include publication date/time and source URL. Final feature eligibility still requires article publication strictly before freeze and source-level verification where material.

reep.reason: Used only to map identities across providers; it must never contribute a football-strength probability by itself.

socceraction.reason: Method code is open, but formal use requires an eligible event feed with point-in-time provenance. Current open-event coverage remains insufficient for formal multi-season promotion.

msoczi_football_predictions.reuse: feature-engineering ideas and multiclass XGBoost architecture only

WorldCupArena.reuse: prospective evidence-package and agent-forecast evaluation ideas

next_gates: Run bounded source smoke tests on GitHub Actions.
Promote only sources with reproducible access and explicit PIT classification.
Build context features as residual inputs, never as free-form probability overrides.
Build a predictability gate separately from the score matrix; target selective accuracy, not all-match 70 percent.
